name: statusline
purpose: Go binary that renders a 3-line Claude Code statusline with 22 widgets covering model, session, context, rate limits, git, weather, GitHub PR/CI, and machine stats.
notes: SoNash version has 22 widgets; JASON-OS port has 16. Six widgets dropped in port: A6 (active agent), B3 (worktree), D1 (hook health), D5 (unacked warnings), F15 (uptime), I4 (session count today). SoNash binary name: sonash-statusline-v2. Go module: sonash-statusline. Cache location: ~/.claude/statusline/cache/ (machine-scoped). config.local.toml committed in SoNash (contains real weather API key — security gap). A compiled binary sonash-statusline-v3.exe is present in the repo (gitignored per .gitignore but file exists as working-tree artifact). sonash-statusline.exe~ tilde-backup also present (gitignored). build.sh adds -a flag only in JASON-OS (force full rebuild).

name: main.go
purpose: Entry point: reads stdin JSON, loads config, calls buildAllWidgets, calls renderLines, then synchronously refreshes API caches post-render.
notes: Contains FlexModel unmarshaler to handle both string (Claude Code >=2.1.89) and object model formats. UTF-8 code page set on Windows via chcp.com. Config dir resolved from executable location, falls back to cwd for go run. Identical in function between SoNash and JASON-OS — only comment differences.

name: widgets.go
purpose: All 22 widget implementations (SoNash) / 16 widget implementations (JASON-OS), organized by I/O type: stdin-only, git shell-out, file-read, API-backed.
notes: SoNash AllWidgets struct has 22 fields: A1,A3,A4,A6,B1,B2,B3,C1,C5,C6,C7,C8,D1,D5,E1,F4,F6,F7,F15,H2,H3,I4. JASON-OS AllWidgets has 16 fields: A1,A3,A4,B1,B2,C1,C5,C6,C7,C8,E1,F4,F6,F7,H2,H3. Dropped in port: A6 (widgetActiveAgent), B3 (widgetWorktree), D1 (widgetHookHealth), D5 (widgetUnackedWarnings), F15 (widgetUptime), I4 (widgetSessionCount). Also removed: hooksOK constant (only needed by D1). JASON-OS widgets.go has no bufio import (D1/D5 used it); tailLastLine/countUnackedSince helpers moved to cache.go or removed. E1 (widgetCurrentTask) retained in JASON-OS.

name: cache.go
purpose: Cache subsystem: manages weather/PR/CI JSON cache files in ~/.claude/statusline/cache/, implements exponential backoff for API failures, fetches OpenWeatherMap and GitHub data.
notes: Classic source=universal / runtime=machine example per Piece 1a §5.3. Source code is universal Go; runtime writes to ~/.claude/statusline/cache/ (machine-scoped). cacheDir() hardcodes ~/.claude/statusline/cache. Backoff state stored in backoff.json. API fetch functions: fetchWeather (OpenWeatherMap current + 24hr forecast), fetchGitHubPR (gh pr view), fetchGitHubCI (gh run list). Identical between SoNash and JASON-OS.

name: config.go
purpose: Config struct definitions and TOML loader; merges config.toml (shared/versioned) with config.local.toml (machine-local override).
notes: Default config hardcodes location=Nashville,TN,US and timezone=America/Chicago — these are sanitize fields for JASON-OS port (already addressed: JASON-OS config.toml retains Nashville defaults in the shared file, left for operator override via config.local.toml). Config loading: defaultConfig() fills all fields, then config.toml overlays, then config.local.toml overlays. Identical between SoNash and JASON-OS.

name: render.go
purpose: Assembles widget results into 3 output lines with ANSI coloring, separator logic, and non-breaking-space substitution for Claude Code terminal compatibility.
notes: SoNash Line 1: A1,B1,B3,B2,A4,C1 (includes worktree B3). JASON-OS Line 1: A1,B1,B2,A4,C1 (B3 dropped). SoNash Line 2: D1,D5,H2,H3,C8 (includes hook health D1, unacked D5). JASON-OS Line 2: H2,H3,C8 (D1,D5 dropped). SoNash Line 3: weatherCluster,F4,A3,F15,I4 (includes uptime F15, session count I4). JASON-OS Line 3: weatherCluster,F4,A3,E1 (F15,I4 dropped; E1 task widget added to line 3). prepareLine and colorize functions identical.

name: build.sh
purpose: Build script: verifies Go, runs tests, compiles binary, installs to ~/.claude/statusline/, copies config files, and runs a test render.
notes: SoNash BINARY_NAME=sonash-statusline-v2, summary says 'Widgets active: 22'. JASON-OS BINARY_NAME=jason-statusline-v2, summary says 'Widgets active: 16'. JASON-OS adds -a flag to go build (force full rebuild). HOME fallback logic handles Windows USERPROFILE via cygpath. Install copies binary + config.toml + config.local.toml to INSTALL_DIR. Step 7 test render uses hardcoded sample JSON — model name differs: SoNash='Opus 4.6', JASON-OS='Opus 4.7'.

name: statusline_test.go
purpose: Go test file covering config loading, stdin parsing, widget rendering, sanitize, cache staleness, countUnackedSince, tailLastLine, prepareLine, and joinWidgets.
notes: SoNash test includes TestCountUnackedSince with 'resolved' entry test case (tests D5 widget logic). JASON-OS test file also includes TestCountUnackedSince even though D5 widget was dropped — countUnackedSince helper must still exist in JASON-OS (likely kept in widgets.go or cache.go for potential future use, or test file was not pruned). Test constant testModelName='Opus 4.6' in SoNash — JASON-OS may differ. 18 test functions total. Tests run via build.sh step 2.

name: go.mod
purpose: Go module definition for the statusline binary.
notes: SoNash: module sonash-statusline, go 1.26.1. JASON-OS: module jason-statusline, go 1.26.1. Single dependency: BurntSushi/toml v1.6.0. Module name is cosmetic only (binary name, not import path for external packages).

name: go.sum
purpose: Go module checksum file locking BurntSushi/toml v1.6.0 dependency.
notes: Standard generated file. Content should be identical between SoNash and JASON-OS since same dependency version. No project-specific data.

name: config.toml
purpose: Shared versioned configuration: widget thresholds, cache intervals, separator, timezone defaults, weather location defaults, and placeholders.
notes: SoNash config.toml and JASON-OS config.toml are functionally identical in structure and values. Both retain Nashville/Chicago defaults — operators override via config.local.toml. SoNash config.toml includes [placeholders] with worktree and agent keys (for dropped A6/B3 widgets); JASON-OS config.toml [placeholders] section omits worktree and agent keys. This is the only structural content difference.

name: config.local.toml.example
purpose: Template for machine-local config overrides; instructs operator to copy to config.local.toml and supply weather API key and optional binary_dir override.
notes: Both SoNash and JASON-OS versions are identical except JASON-OS .example wording slightly cleaned ('another machine' vs 'second machine'). Placeholder value 'YOUR_OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY' is correct in both .example files. Binary_dir default ~/.claude/statusline identical in both.

name: config.local.toml
purpose: Machine-local overrides: weather API key and optional binary_dir. Gitignored in BOTH SoNash and JASON-OS (verified via git check-ignore).
notes: Working-tree-only file containing a real API key (value REDACTED from research finding). Verified gitignored in both repos (git ls-files --error-unmatch returns not-tracked). Initial D13 agent report of 'committed in SoNash' was INCORRECT — post-agent verification confirmed gitignored. Recommend rotating key at openweathermap.org as routine hygiene. No git history scrub required.

name: .gitignore
purpose: Gitignore for statusline directory: excludes config.local.toml, compiled binaries, and cache/ directory.
notes: SoNash .gitignore patterns: config.local.toml, sonash-statusline, sonash-statusline.exe, statusline.exe, *.exe~, cache/. The pattern 'sonash-statusline' (no extension) would catch the Unix binary; 'sonash-statusline.exe' for Windows. The v3 binary (sonash-statusline-v3.exe) is NOT covered by these patterns — it appears in the working tree as an unignored file. JASON-OS has no separate .gitignore in tools/statusline/ — the root .gitignore likely covers it. This file exists only in SoNash.

name: sonash-statusline-v3.exe
purpose: Compiled Windows binary for SoNash statusline v3 (working-tree artifact, machine-specific).
notes: Generated binary. Not covered by .gitignore (patterns only cover sonash-statusline and sonash-statusline.exe, not sonash-statusline-v3.exe). This may be an accidentally untracked file or version increment beyond what .gitignore accounts for. Version naming convention (v3) is inconsistent with build.sh which builds 'sonash-statusline-v2'. Suggests manual rename or older build artifact. Should not be committed.

name: sonash-statusline.exe~
purpose: Tilde-backup of a previous compiled Windows binary (editor or shell artifact).
notes: Covered by *.exe~ pattern in .gitignore. Likely created by the statusline rebuild safety protocol (never overwrite running exe in-place; bump versioned filename + shim pointer). Safe to delete. Present only in SoNash working tree.

name: sonash-statusline
purpose: Compiled Unix/Linux binary for SoNash statusline (working-tree artifact, no extension).
notes: Covered by 'sonash-statusline' pattern in .gitignore. Likely built on a Unix machine or WSL. The actual install binary used on this Windows machine is the .exe variant installed at ~/.claude/statusline/. Safe to delete from working tree.
